title: [PDF] Supervisory Stress Test Documentation Credit Risk Models
summary: This Federal Reserve document provides technical documentation of credit risk models used in the 2026 Supervisory Stress Test, covering Corporate, Commercial Real Estate, First Lien Mortgage, Home Equity, Credit Card, Auto, and Other Retail model categories. The January 2026 revision adds transparency on model coefficients and technical corrections. This is granular supervisory methodology documentation that does not address market structure or macro catalysts directly.
key_points: Documents credit risk models used for 2026 Supervisory Stress Test across seven retail and commercial categories
Updated January 2026 to add transparency on regression coefficients and make technical corrections
Covers model components including PD (Probability of Default), LGD (Loss Given Default), and related specifications
Includes Corporate Model, CRE Model, First Lien Mortgage Model, Home Equity Model, Credit Card Model, Auto Model, and Other Retail Model
Document provides technical model specifications including spline knot implementations and estimation sample windows
Source is targeted at bank compliance and regulatory supervision, not market participants
why_it_matters: This document is relevant to bank regulators and large financial institutions subject to stress testing requirements. It has no direct bearing on market microstructure, trading operations, or macro catalysts. It is granular technical documentation that would not be useful for market structure or macro catalyst research.

issues_found: SOURCE METADATA TARGET 'market structure' does not match document content - this is supervisory stress test model documentation, not market structure research
Document is regulatory methodology for bank stress testing (DFAST/CCAR), not market microstructure or trading analysis
Record correctly identifies lack of market relevance in why_it_matters but the system still generated and attempted to archive this record
topic field assigned as 'market structure' in record contradicts the document's actual content
Source has no bearing on market microstructure, trading operations, or macro catalysts as the record itself acknowledges

human_review.notes: The source metadata incorrectly tagged this as 'market structure' TARGET, and the system generated a research record for content that has no relevance to market structure or macro catalysts. The document is internal Federal Reserve supervisory methodology for credit risk models used in stress testing - designed for bank compliance officers, not market analysts. While the document is from a high-trust domain and is technically accurate, it fundamentally does not belong in a market structure research archive. The generated record properly identifies this mismatch but still processes it for archiving.

notes: SOURCE NOT RELEVANT to market structure or macro catalysts. Document is internal supervisory methodology for stress test credit risk models. While from a high-trust domain (federalreserve.gov), the content addresses bank regulatory compliance methodology rather than market operations, microstructure, or macro catalysts. Recommend topic reassignment to 'regulatory/supervisory' or exclusion from archive. The PDF is a reference document for DFAST/CCAR model specifications with no direct market implications.
